_doc: Raven Hollow calendar (BACKLOG #28). Consumed by CalendarSystem (scripts/systems/calendar_system.gd). The Draconian year is 12 months x 30 days = 360 days, one game-day per DayNight cycle. Twelve seasonal festivals from design/CALENDAR_EVENTS.md, each gated by a date window on this calendar (WoW holiday cadence adapted to Draconia lore). 'window' ordinals wrap the year; 'recurring' fires on the same day-of-month every month (the Grey Fair). Month names are functional calendar labels tied to the festivals/seasons; the festival names + lore are canon (CALENDAR_EVENTS.md). 'buff' folds a small themed stat modifier onto the player while active (StatsSystem source 'calendar:<id>').

events.name: The Kept Names
events.title: the Remembered
events.notify: The Kept Names begins. Read the dead aloud; a name kept warm is a name the North cannot claim.
events.blurb: In the coldest stretch, every settlement reads its dead aloud, all the way back. Lanterns at the graves; coins pressed into headstones.

events.name: The Courting of Secrets
events.title: the Confidant
events.notify: The Courting of Secrets begins. Every valentine is a hostage negotiation.
events.blurb: Blestem's great game in the open: sealed letters change hands, each carrying one true secret about its sender. Trust is the currency, not flowers.

events.name: Sowing Day
events.title: Sower
events.notify: Sowing Day begins. Hide the seed-stones where even you won't look.
events.blurb: Painted seed-stones are hidden in the furrows by night and hunted at dawn. Chaos by hand, a hedge against a machine that arranges famines.

events.name: Ward's Week
events.title: Week-Warden
events.notify: Ward's Week begins. Show a child one true thing about the world.
events.blurb: Every ward of the orphanage is assigned a week-warden who must walk them through Draconia. Strategy and mercy both, pure Fielderine.

events.name: The Emberfall Vigil
events.hub: The Bent Oar landing, Iron Vein
events.title: Vigil-Keeper
events.notify: The Emberfall Vigil begins. We make our own warmth to remember what honest warmth is.
events.blurb: The longest days, and Draconia's most important lie: honest fires that re-train the skin to feel the difference between hearth-heat and stone-heat.

events.name: The Proofing
events.title: Proofed
events.notify: The Proofing begins. Flat beer is a symptom; good beer is a clean bill of health.
events.blurb: The Underlanguage kills yeast, so brewing is divination. Every village brings its ale to be judged and mapped by the accidental early-warning network.

events.name: The Gift-Tide
events.title: of the Tide
events.notify: The Gift-Tide begins. This year's bread is last war's dead. Set the empty chair.
events.blurb: The week the continent honors the fallen who feed you. An empty chair is set at every table, for the ones in the ground -- all of them.

events.name: The Thinning
events.title: the Hollow
events.notify: The Thinning begins. Wear a mask so the thing behind the Veil cannot learn your face.
events.blurb: The fortnight the Veil sags. Masks are worn not to frighten spirits but to hide your face; wicker Hollow Men burn at crossroads.

events.name: The Table of Strangers
events.title: Table-Setter
events.notify: The Table of Strangers begins. Any table set outdoors must feed whoever sits at it, no names asked.
events.blurb: Hospitality as counter-magic, one bowl at a time -- the anti-famine, the anti-Debt-Pit. Watch who sits down; some strangers are the hungering.

events.name: The Long Vigil Feast
events.title: Keeper of the Vigil
events.notify: The Long Vigil Feast begins. Leave the door unlatched for the Grey Guest, and set his bowl.
events.blurb: Midwinter: Draconia celebrates the only victory it ever won -- still being here. A bowl is set at every table for the Grey Guest, who is you.

events.name: The Turning of the Count
events.title: Who Counts
events.notify: The Turning of the Count. Every bell, hammer, and horn goes silent, and the whole continent listens. Count the notes.
events.blurb: One minute at midnight of the year's last day. Three notes: the Pause holds, another year bought. Cheer three for luck.

events.name: The Grey Fair
events.hub: Riverfork docks (a pier that isn't usually there)
events.title: Fairgoer
events.notify: The Grey Fair has moored at Riverfork. Games of chance with unsettling prizes; a fortune-teller always accurate, never helpful.
events.blurb: First week of every month, a fair that wasn't there the week before. Collector-era leakage, drifting up the era-crossing route with goods a thousand years early.
